parsed.summary: The commit renames the npm scope and adds an interactive menu to the setup command when already configured. The scope rename from @anthropic to @anthropic-ai (or similar) in package.json is a breaking change for existing users who depend on the old package name — no migration path or deprecation notice is provided. The interactive menu addition in commands/setup.md lacks error handling for edge cases (non-interactive environments, unexpected input). However, since these are primarily documentation/config changes for a Claude plugin, the blast radius is limited. The most concerning issue is the scope rename without migration guidance.

findings.title: npm scope rename without migration path or compatibility shim
findings.body: The package.json changes the npm scope/name. Any existing installation references, CI pipelines, or documentation pointing to the old scope will break silently. There is no deprecation notice, no migration guide, and no backward-compatible alias mentioned in the diff. Users upgrading from v0.4.6 will find their existing installs orphaned.
findings.recommendation: Add a migration note in CHANGELOG.md and release_card.md documenting the scope change, including uninstall of the old package and reinstall of the new one. Consider publishing a stub under the old name that redirects or warns.

findings.title: Interactive menu in setup command has no non-interactive fallback or timeout handling
findings.body: The commands/setup.md now includes an interactive menu when already configured. If this is invoked in a non-TTY environment (CI, piped input, headless), the menu will hang or produce undefined behavior. No --yes, --skip, or timeout flag is documented for scripted use.
findings.recommendation: Document a non-interactive flag or default behavior for automated/scripted environments. Ensure the command degrades gracefully when stdin is not a TTY.

findings.title: CHANGELOG entry references wrong prior version tag
findings.body: The CHANGELOG.md references version v0.4.7 but the user focus references 'C1-v044-setup-menu' and the prior context suggests v0.4.4 lineage. The version numbering may be inconsistent, which can confuse automated release tooling or consumers who parse the changelog.
findings.recommendation: Verify that the version in CHANGELOG.md matches the actual release version being prepared and that the prior version reference is correct.

next_steps: Confirm the npm scope change is intentional and publish migration instructions before release.
Test the setup command in a non-interactive/CI environment to verify it doesn't hang.
Verify CHANGELOG version numbers are consistent with the actual release being cut.
